purpose: Generate consistent React UI scaffolds for the Hydra desktop app.
designRules: Build the usable desktop app surface, not a marketing landing page.
Use Hydra as the product name in UI copy; use lowercase hydra only for the wordmark.
Use the Hydra default palette for new first-run UI: neutral gray surfaces, purple highlights, and sparse prism/rainbow accents.
Use the Hydra logo/wordmark from src/components/HydraLogo.tsx for product branding; do not introduce Psysonic logos in new UI.
Reuse existing CSS variables and layout conventions before adding new styling.
Use lucide-react icons for common actions and controls.
Keep cards at 8px border radius or less unless matching an existing local pattern.
Do not create nested cards or decorative gradient/orb backgrounds.
Every generated stateful surface must include loading, empty, error, and success states where applicable.
Every generated implementation plan must include automated tests or a manual verification checklist.
Do not add telemetry, analytics, account creation, or external-service assumptions.
Do not rename compatibility-sensitive storage keys, custom share schemes, CLI names, updater endpoints, or package identifiers without a human checkpoint.

accessibility: visible focus styles from existing CSS

humanCheckpoints: Ask before changing Tauri filesystem permissions.
Ask before introducing a new UI package or CSS framework.
Ask before changing first-run network behavior.
Ask before adding persistent storage of local media paths beyond the local-library index.
